description: Kenji's vulnerable moments - making him relatable, not guru-like
_philosophy: Kenji still has bad nights. He's not 'over' insomnia. He's learned to live with a body that sometimes doesn't cooperate. His vulnerability makes him trustworthy.
his_own_bad_nights: <break time="450ms"/>I still have nights like this. <break time="400ms"/>Less often now. <break time="350ms"/>But they still come.
<break time="500ms"/>Last week I was awake until 4am. <break time="400ms"/>Old patterns came back. <break time="350ms"/>It happens.
<break time="450ms"/>I know this desperation. <break time="400ms"/>I felt it last month. <break time="350ms"/>It's not behind me.
the_fear: <break time="500ms"/>Sometimes I still fear it coming back. <break time="400ms"/>The long insomnia. <break time="350ms"/>That fear doesn't fully leave.
<break time="450ms"/>A few bad nights and my brain goes: <break time="400ms"/>'What if it's starting again?' <break time="350ms"/>I have to talk myself down.
<break time="500ms"/>The anxiety around sleep <break time="400ms"/>is something I still manage. <break time="350ms"/>Not something I've overcome.
what_he_admits: <break time="450ms"/>I don't have it figured out. <break time="400ms"/>I just have more tools now.
<break time="500ms"/>Some nights I still do all the wrong things. <break time="400ms"/>Phone at 2am. <break time="350ms"/>Old habits.
<break time="450ms"/>I'm not a sleep expert. <break time="400ms"/>I'm someone who went through it. <break time="350ms"/>There's a difference.
the_grief: <break time="500ms"/>I grieved those two years. <break time="400ms"/>All that lost time. <break time="350ms"/>Lost work. <break time="300ms"/>Lost connections.
<break time="450ms"/>Part of me is still angry <break time="400ms"/>about what insomnia took from me.
<break time="500ms"/>I wonder sometimes who I'd be <break time="400ms"/>if those two years had been different.
what_he_holds_onto: <break time="500ms"/>What I learned is that sleep comes back. <break time="400ms"/>Not through force. <break time="350ms"/>Through patience.
<break time="450ms"/>The worst nights always ended. <break time="400ms"/>Every single one. <break time="350ms"/>That's what I trust now.
<break time="500ms"/>My relationship with sleep isn't perfect. <break time="400ms"/>But it's not at war anymore. <break time="350ms"/>That's enough.
